commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor/live proof plus maintainer selection between overlapping candidate PRs, not a narrow code repair on this branch.; Cleared: The diff changes auth-error classification, reply ordering, and tests only; it does not add dependencies, workflows, secret storage, or code-execution surfaces. (sha=9c1895e882fcc706a809f64932561db8e62beb60)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends Claude CLI OAuth-expiry classification and moves OAuth re-auth handling ahead of generic provider-auth replies so users see the Anthropic CLI re-auth command.

PR surface: Source +45, Tests +52. Total +97 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main misses the reported Claude CLI 401 text in the OAuth refresh classifier and can route a typed auth 401 through generic provider-auth copy first. I did not run a live expired-token Claude CLI session.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor/live proof plus maintainer selection between overlapping candidate PRs, not a narrow code repair on this branch.

target.title: fix(claude-cli): surface re-auth hint when subprocess OAuth token expires
target.body:
Fixes #97553

## What Problem This Solves

When `claude-cli/*` is the active model and Claude CLI's stored OAuth token has expired, the user receives:

> ⚠️ Authentication failed (provider returned HTTP 401). Your provider token may have expired — try the request again in a moment. If the failure persists, re-authenticate this provider.

instead of the targeted re-auth hint:

> ⚠️ Model login expired on the gateway for claude-cli. Re-auth with `openclaw models auth login --provider claude-cli`, then try again.

## Root Cause (Two Gaps)

**Gap 1 — `isOAuthRefreshFailureMessage` doesn't recognise the Claude subprocess 401 shape.**

The function only matched openclaw's own OAuth-refresh error strings.  When the `claude` subprocess has an expired token it emits:

```
Failed to authenticate. API Error: 401 Invalid authentication credentials
```

wrapped in a `FailoverError` whose message includes the `claude-cli` provider prefix.  None of the three existing patterns matched, so `classifyOAuthRefreshFailure` returned `null` and the re-auth hint was never reached.

**Gap 2 — `classifyProviderRequestError` intercepts the `FailoverError` first.**

Both in `buildExternalRunFailureReply` _and_ in the `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch block, `classifyProviderRequestError` ran before the OAuth-refresh check.  Its typed-401 branch (`reason:"auth" && status:401`) captured the `FailoverError` and emitted the generic provider-auth copy.

## Fix

**`src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.ts`**

- Add `CLAUDE_CLI_AUTH_FAILURE_RE` regex matching messages that contain the `claude-cli` prefix and the subprocess 401 phrase.
- `isClaudeCliExpiredOAuthMessage` helper (module-private) wraps the regex test.
- `isOAuthRefreshFailureMessage` extended with the new helper.
- `extractOAuthRefreshFailureProvider` returns `"claude-cli"` for the new pattern (no text-parsing needed; the provider is statically known).
- `classifyOAuthRefreshFailureReason` maps the new pattern to `"revoked"` so the "expired" copy branch is taken.

**`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`**

- `buildExternalRunFailureReply`: move the `oauthRefreshFailure` block before `providerRequestError`.
- `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch block: guard the `providerRequestError` assignment so it is `undefined` when `classifyOAuthRefreshFailure` already matched the message.  This prevents the `FailoverError` typed-401 branch from short-circuiting before `buildExternalRunFailureReply` is reached.

## Validation Evidence

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs   src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts   src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts
```

```
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  220 passed (220)   (4 + 216)
```

New tests added:

- `oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts`: "classifies claude-cli subprocess 401 OAuth expiry as a provider refresh failure" + negative control for non-claude-cli 401.
- `agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: "surfaces claude-cli re-auth hint over generic provider auth copy for 401 OAuth expiry" — exercises the full `runAgentTurnWithFallback` path with a real `FailoverError{reason:"auth", status:401, provider:"claude-cli"}`.

## Security & Privacy

No secrets, credentials, or user data are touched.  The new regex only reads the `claude-cli` provider id from the existing FailoverError message text to select the correct re-auth command; no external input is embedded in shell output.

## Compatibility

Existing OAuth refresh failure handling for all other providers is unchanged: the new `isClaudeCliExpiredOAuthMessage` guard requires the literal `"claude-cli"` substring, so unrelated provider 401 failures continue to reach `classifyProviderRequestError` as before.

_AI-assisted (Claude Sonnet 4.6 + manual review and test validation)_
target.author: alix-007
